findings.title: Blog post page titles can exceed 60 characters
findings.description: The blog post template generates titles using the pattern `${post.data.title} — Analogify Studio Journal`. With the current post, this produces an 83-character title: 'Building Analogify Studio: Where Photography Meets Code — Analogify Studio Journal'. Google typically truncates titles beyond ~60 characters in search results, losing the brand suffix and potentially cutting important keywords.
findings.evidence: title={`${post.data.title} — Analogify Studio Journal`}
findings.impact: Truncated titles in SERPs reduce click-through rates and can lose brand visibility. The most important keywords should appear in the first 60 characters.
findings.fix_suggestion: Shorten the suffix to just '| Analogify' or dynamically truncate. Example: title={`${post.data.title} | Analogify`} which keeps the title under 60 chars for most post titles.

findings.title: Missing BreadcrumbList structured data on sub-pages
findings.description: The features, blog index, blog posts, and privacy pages all lack BreadcrumbList schema markup. Breadcrumbs help search engines understand site hierarchy and can appear as rich results in SERPs, improving click-through rates.
findings.impact: Missing breadcrumb rich results in SERPs. Search engines have less signal about site hierarchy. Pages like /features and /blog/hello-world don't communicate their position in the site structure to crawlers.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add automatic BreadcrumbList generation in Base.astro based on Astro.url.pathname. Generate path segments and create ListItem entries for each level of the URL hierarchy.

findings.title: demo.html canonical URL mismatches og:url
findings.description: The demo.html page has conflicting URL signals: the canonical link uses 'https://codyjo.com/demo.html' (with .html extension) while og:url uses 'https://codyjo.com/demo' (without extension). While the page is noindexed, these signals should still be consistent.
findings.evidence:
<link rel="canonical" href="https://codyjo.com/demo.html" />
...
<meta property="og:url" content="https://codyjo.com/demo" />
findings.impact: Conflicting URL signals confuse social platforms and any crawler that encounters the page despite noindex. Minor impact since the page is blocked from indexing.
findings.fix_suggestion: Make both URLs consistent. Use 'https://codyjo.com/demo.html' for both canonical and og:url, or standardize on /demo if CloudFront handles the rewrite.

findings.title: Article schema missing dateModified
findings.description: The blog post Article schema includes datePublished but not dateModified. Google uses dateModified to determine content freshness, which is a ranking signal.
findings.impact: Missing freshness signal for Google's Article understanding. Updated articles without dateModified may not get the freshness boost they deserve.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add 'dateModified': post.data.lastModified || post.data.date to the Article schema. Optionally add lastModified to the blog frontmatter schema.

findings.title: Missing og:image:width and og:image:height meta tags
findings.description: The Base layout includes og:image but not og:image:width and og:image:height. Without these, social platforms must download the full image to determine dimensions before rendering the preview.
findings.evidence:
<meta property="og:image" content={new URL(ogImage, Astro.site).href} />
<!-- Missing og:image:width and og:image:height -->
findings.impact: Social sharing previews may render incorrectly on first share. Facebook debugger and Twitter Card validator will flag this as a warning.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add after the og:image tag:
<meta property="og:image:width" content="1200" />
<meta property="og:image:height" content="630" />

findings.title: Missing Apple touch icon
findings.description: No <link rel='apple-touch-icon'> is defined in the document head. When users bookmark the site on iOS or add it to their home screen, they'll see a generic screenshot instead of a branded icon.
findings.evidence:
<link rel="icon" type="image/svg+xml" href="/favicon.svg" />
<!-- No apple-touch-icon -->
findings.impact: Poor mobile bookmark/home screen experience on iOS. Minor SEO signal but affects brand presentation.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Create a 180x180 PNG icon and add:
<link rel="apple-touch-icon" sizes="180x180" href="/apple-touch-icon.png" />

findings.title: Blog index title slightly exceeds 60 characters
findings.description: The blog index page title 'Journal — Photography, AI & Creative Tech | Analogify Studio' is approximately 61 characters, slightly exceeding the recommended 50-60 character limit.
findings.evidence: title="Journal — Photography, AI & Creative Tech | Analogify Studio"
findings.impact: Title may be truncated by 1-2 characters in Google SERPs. Very minor.
findings.fix_suggestion: Shorten to 'Journal — Photography & Creative Tech | Analogify' (52 chars).

findings.title: Missing FAQPage schema on features comparison content
findings.description: The features page contains a detailed comparison table that implicitly answers common questions (Does Analogify have automatic grading? Video delivery? etc.). This content would benefit from FAQPage schema markup, which directly feeds voice assistants and AI search engines (Bing Chat, Google SGE, ChatGPT browsing).
findings.evidence:
<section class="section comparison-section" id="compare">
  <!-- Comparison table answering common feature questions -->
  <!-- No FAQPage schema -->
findings.impact: Missing FAQ rich results in SERPs and AI search surfaces. FAQ schema has high visibility in featured snippets and is directly consumed by AI assistants.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add FAQPage schema to the features page with Q&A pairs derived from the comparison table. Pass via the schema prop to Base layout.

findings.title: No dedicated author/about page for E-E-A-T signals
findings.description: Google's E-E-A-T (Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, Trustworthiness) guidelines are increasingly important. The site has a brief 'Story' section on the homepage but no dedicated about/author page establishing Cody Jo's credentials in photography and software development.
findings.evidence:
The Story

Born from the
darkroom
findings.impact: Weaker E-E-A-T signals for AI search engines and Google's quality raters. Author authority pages help AI models attribute content to credible sources.
findings.fix_suggestion: Create /src/pages/about.astro with a dedicated author bio page. Include professional background, photography experience, technical expertise, and Person schema with sameAs links.

findings.title: No IndexNow integration for rapid content discovery
findings.description: IndexNow is a protocol that instantly notifies search engines (Bing, Yandex, Naver) when content is published or updated. Without it, new blog posts rely on crawl schedules for discovery.
findings.impact: New blog posts and page updates aren't instantly discoverable by Bing and other IndexNow-supporting engines. Minor impact for infrequent publishing.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add an IndexNow key file at /public/{key}.txt and submit URLs on deploy via CI/CD. Alternatively, use an Astro IndexNow integration.

findings.title: Missing speakable schema for voice search optimization
findings.description: The site doesn't include speakable schema (SpeakableSpecification) on any pages. Speakable markup identifies content sections best suited for text-to-speech, helping voice assistants deliver more relevant answers.
findings.impact: Voice search queries about 'photo gallery platform' or 'automatic film grading' won't surface this site's content as a speakable answer.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add speakable property to the homepage SoftwareApplication schema:
"speakable": { "@type": "SpeakableSpecification", "cssSelector": [".hero-tagline", ".hero-desc"] }

findings.title: Minimal blog content limits AI entity establishment
findings.description: The blog has only 1 published post. AI search engines build entity understanding and topical authority from a body of related content. With minimal content, the site has limited signals for establishing authority.
findings.evidence: Only 1 blog post found in src/content/blog/
findings.impact: Limited topical authority signals for AI search engines. Competing sites with more content on photography platforms and film grading will have stronger entity associations.
findings.fix_suggestion: Publish additional blog posts covering key topics: ChromaHaus technical deep-dive, comparison with other gallery platforms, photography workflow tips. Aim for 5-10 posts to establish topical authority.

findings.title: No about/mentions schema for entity relationships
findings.description: The structured data doesn't use schema.org's about or mentions properties to explicitly define entity relationships between Analogify Studio, ChromaHaus, photography, and color grading.
findings.impact: AI models have fewer explicit signals for understanding entity relationships. Content structure is good enough for inference, but explicit schema is stronger.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add about and mentions to the SoftwareApplication schema:
"about": [{ "@type": "Thing", "name": "Photography" }, { "@type": "Thing", "name": "Color grading" }],
"mentions": [{ "@type": "SoftwareApplication", "name": "ChromaHaus" }]

findings.title: demo.html outside Astro build pipeline uses Google Fonts CDN
findings.description: The demo.html page is a standalone HTML file in /public that bypasses the Astro build pipeline. It loads fonts from Google Fonts CDN (fonts.googleapis.com) while the rest of the site self-hosts fonts for GDPR compliance. This creates an inconsistency and a third-party data transfer on the demo page.
findings.evidence:
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>
<link rel="preload" as="style" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Playfair+Display:ital,wght@0,400;...&display=swap">
findings.impact: Third-party data transfer to Google on demo page visits. Inconsistent with the site's GDPR-compliant self-hosted font strategy.
findings.fix_suggestion: Migrate demo.html to src/pages/demo.astro (as noted in the file's TODO comment) so it uses the same self-hosted fonts and Astro build pipeline. Until then, replace Google Fonts CDN links with references to /fonts/.

findings.title: Privacy policy incorrectly lists Google Fonts as a third-party service
findings.description: Section 7 of the privacy policy states 'Google Fonts: Web typography (loaded from Google's CDN)' and Section 7a mentions Google Fonts data transfer to the US. However, the main site self-hosts all fonts — only the standalone demo.html uses Google Fonts CDN. This creates a misleading privacy disclosure.
findings.evidence: • Google Fonts: Web typography (loaded from Google's CDN)
findings.impact: Inaccurate privacy policy undermines user trust. Could be flagged in a GDPR audit. Users may be unnecessarily concerned about Google data transfer.
findings.fix_suggestion: Either remove the Google Fonts reference after migrating demo.html to Astro, or clarify: 'Google Fonts: Used only on the demo page. The main site uses self-hosted fonts with no third-party data transfer.'

findings.title: Footer navigation lacks semantic <nav> element
findings.description: The footer contains two navigation columns ('Navigate' and 'Connect') wrapped in plain <div> elements instead of semantic <nav> elements. The primary navigation in the header correctly uses role='navigation', but the footer links aren't identified as navigation.
findings.evidence:
Navigate

Story
findings.impact: Search engines may miss footer links as navigation signals. Screen readers can't identify these link groups as navigation landmarks.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Wrap the footer link columns in a <nav> element:
<nav class="footer-cols" aria-label="Footer navigation">

findings.title: Gallery strip has 12 duplicate image alt texts due to animation looping
findings.description: The gallery strip duplicates 6 images (12 total in the DOM) for seamless CSS animation looping. Each duplicate has identical alt text to its counterpart, meaning crawlers see each alt text twice.
findings.impact: Minor dilution of alt text signals. Crawlers see 12 images with only 6 unique alt descriptions.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add aria-hidden='true' to the duplicated image set and use empty alt="" on duplicates since they're purely decorative repeats:

findings.title: All 17 font files served as TTF instead of WOFF2
findings.description: All font files (Playfair Display, Inter, JetBrains Mono, Cormorant Garamond) are served in TTF format. WOFF2 achieves 30-50% smaller file sizes through Brotli compression. The current 17 TTF files total approximately 3.6 MB; WOFF2 could reduce this to ~1.5-2 MB. This directly impacts LCP and FCP.
findings.evidence:
@font-face { font-family: 'Playfair Display'; font-style: normal; font-weight: 400; font-display: swap; src: url('/fonts/playfair-400.ttf') format('truetype'); }
/* ... 16 more TTF font-face declarations */
findings.impact: ~1.5-2 MB of unnecessary font data transferred on every first visit. Significantly impacts Core Web Vitals (LCP, FCP), especially on mobile. Google uses CWV as a ranking signal.

findings.title: No font preloading for critical above-the-fold fonts
findings.description: The Inter (body) and Playfair Display (headings) fonts are not preloaded. These are critical for rendering above-the-fold content. Without preloading, the browser discovers them only after parsing CSS, causing a Flash of Unstyled Text (FOUT).
findings.evidence:
<!-- Fonts: self-hosted (no third-party data transfer) -->
<!-- No <link rel="preload"> for critical fonts -->
findings.impact: Delayed font rendering affects LCP. Hero heading and body text may flash with fallback fonts before custom fonts load, especially on slower connections.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add preload hints for the 2-3 most critical font files:
<link rel="preload" href="/fonts/playfair-400.ttf" as="font" type="font/ttf" crossorigin />
<link rel="preload" href="/fonts/inter-300.ttf" as="font" type="font/ttf" crossorigin />
After WOFF2 conversion (SEO-018), update to reference .woff2 files.

findings.title: Images lack srcset/sizes for responsive delivery
findings.description: All images serve the same large file (1600px+) to all viewports. Gallery strip images display at 350px wide on desktop (260px on mobile) but download the full 1600px image. No srcset or sizes attributes are used.
findings.impact: Mobile users download 3-5x more image data than needed. Impacts LCP, page weight, and data usage. Google PageSpeed will flag 'Properly size images'.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Use Astro's built-in <Image /> component for automatic responsive image generation, or generate multiple sizes and use srcset/sizes:
srcset="/images/hero-center-400.webp 400w, /images/hero-center-800.webp 800w, /images/hero-center-1600.webp 1600w"
sizes="(max-width: 600px) 260px, 350px"

findings.title: Nav scroll listener lacks explicit passive flag
findings.description: The scroll event listener in Nav.astro doesn't specify { passive: true }. While modern browsers often default to passive, explicitly marking it ensures consistent scroll performance. The homepage parallax listener correctly uses { passive: true } but the nav listener doesn't.
findings.impact: Potential scroll jank on older browsers that don't default to passive scroll listeners. Minor INP impact.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add { passive: true } to the listener:
window.addEventListener('scroll', () => { ... }, { passive: true });

findings.title: Film grain SVG overlay applied to entire viewport may affect mobile performance
findings.description: A full-viewport film grain effect is applied via body::after with an inline SVG feTurbulence filter. SVG filter effects on the entire viewport can be GPU-intensive on low-end mobile devices, potentially affecting scroll performance and INP.
findings.impact: May cause GPU-heavy rendering on low-end devices. The effect is at 3% opacity — the visual benefit may not justify the performance cost on mobile.

findings.title: Missing og:image:alt meta tag
findings.description: The Open Graph meta tags include og:image but not og:image:alt. While optional, og:image:alt improves accessibility of shared content on social platforms and is recommended by the Open Graph Protocol.
findings.evidence:
<meta property="og:image" content={new URL(ogImage, Astro.site).href} />
<meta property="og:locale" content="en_US" />
<!-- Missing og:image:alt -->
findings.impact: Social platform screen readers can't describe the shared image to visually impaired users. Minor accessibility and social sharing quality impact.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add og:image:alt prop to Base.astro:
ogImageAlt = 'Analogify Studio — Intelligent photo gallery platform'
<meta property="og:image:alt" content={ogImageAlt} />
